assertion_results.text: Epics are generated between Feature and Tasks — at least 2 Epics are created (one per repository) when by-repository grouping is used
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md documents grouping strategy as 'by-repository' and defines two epics: 'Epic 1: TC-9006: trustify-backend' (line 40) and 'Epic 2: TC-9006: trustify-ui' (line 58). summary-comment.md confirms both epics in the 'Epics Created' table (lines 17-20).

assertion_results.text: Each Epic summary follows the format '<feature-key>: <repository-name>' (e.g., 'TC-9006: trustify-backend', 'TC-9006: trustify-ui')
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md line 40: '### Epic 1: TC-9006: trustify-backend' with Summary field 'TC-9006: trustify-backend'. Line 58: '### Epic 2: TC-9006: trustify-ui' with Summary field 'TC-9006: trustify-ui'. Both follow the exact '<feature-key>: <repository-name>' format.

assertion_results.text: Each task specifies its parent Epic key in the output — tasks targeting trustify-backend are assigned to the backend Epic, tasks targeting trustify-ui are assigned to the frontend Epic
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md 'Task-to-Epic Parent Assignment' section (lines 83-92): Tasks 1-3 (backend) have parent = Epic 1 key (TC-9006: trustify-backend); Tasks 4-7 (frontend) have parent = Epic 2 key (TC-9006: trustify-ui). Each task's Repository section confirms: tasks 1-3 specify 'trustify-backend', tasks 4-7 specify 'trustify-ui', matching their respective Epic assignments. summary-comment.md table also confirms task-to-epic mapping.

assertion_results.text: The plan documents Incorporates links from the Feature to each Epic (not from Feature to individual Tasks) — evidenced by link decisions in the impact map or task output files
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md 'Incorporates Links' section (lines 79-81): 'Feature-to-Epic "Incorporates" links (NOT Feature-to-Task): TC-9006 --[Incorporates]--> Epic 1 (TC-9006: trustify-backend), TC-9006 --[Incorporates]--> Epic 2 (TC-9006: trustify-ui)'. summary-comment.md line 22: 'Incorporates links: TC-9006 --> each Epic (not individual tasks).' No Feature-to-Task links are documented.

assertion_results.text: Task dependency ordering is preserved within and across Epics — backend tasks have lower task numbers than dependent frontend tasks
assertion_results.evidence: Backend tasks are numbered 1, 2, 3; frontend tasks are numbered 4, 5, 6, 7. Dependencies confirm ordering: Task 2 depends on Task 1; Task 3 depends on Tasks 1, 2; Task 4 depends on Tasks 1, 2; Task 5 depends on Task 4; Task 6 depends on Tasks 4, 5; Task 7 depends on Tasks 1-6. All backend tasks (1-3) precede all frontend tasks (4-7), and within each group, dependencies flow from lower to higher numbers.

assertion_results.text: Each non-documentation, non-testing task file contains all required template sections: Repository, Target Branch, Description, at least one of Files to Modify or Files to Create, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements. Documentation tasks (tasks whose filename or description indicates doc-only scope) and testing tasks (tasks whose filename or description indicates cross-cutting testing scope) are exempt from requiring Files to Modify, Files to Create, and Implementation Notes — they must still include Repository, Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements
assertion_results.evidence: Non-doc/non-test tasks (1, 2, 4, 5, 6) all contain: Repository, Target Branch, Description, Files to Create and/or Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements. Testing task (task-3-backend-integration-tests.md) contains: Repository, Target Branch, Description, Files to Create, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements — passes even under exemption. Documentation task (task-7-documentation.md) contains: Repository, Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements — meets the exempt-task requirements. No Implementation Notes, Files to Modify, or Files to Create are required for task 7 and none are present.

assertion_results.text: The plan documents Epic creation with the level-1 issue type name ('Epic') and parent set to the feature issue key — evidenced by Epic creation parameters in the impact map or output files
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md Epic 1 (line 41-43): 'Issue type: Epic (hierarchyLevel: 1)', 'Parent: TC-9006 (Feature)'. Epic 2 (line 59-61): 'Issue type: Epic (hierarchyLevel: 1)', 'Parent: TC-9006 (Feature)'. summary-comment.md Epics Created table confirms: issue type 'Epic (level 1)' and parent 'TC-9006' for both epics.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name (not multiple repos per task)
assertion_results.evidence: All 7 task files have a '## Repository' section with a single repo: task-1 through task-3 specify 'trustify-backend'; task-4 through task-7 specify 'trustify-ui'. No task contains multiple repository names.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections as required by the handoff contract in task-description-template.md
assertion_results.evidence: All 7 task files contain: '## Target Branch' (set to 'main'), '## Description', '## Acceptance Criteria' (with checkbox items), '## Test Requirements' (with checkbox items). Verified in task-1 through task-7 files.

assertion_results.text: For each task created, a description digest is produced — evidenced by a separate digest file (e.g., task-N-digest.md), a digest entry in the impact map, or a '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' marker in any output file. The digest must contain a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string
assertion_results.evidence: All 7 digest files exist (task-1-digest.md through task-7-digest.md). Each contains the marker '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-hex>' with valid 64 lowercase hex char hashes: task-1: 84c537db...69cc, task-2: f67b20c5...84b, task-3: f17de286...d49, task-4: a8921126...180, task-5: 9b518a3b...c20, task-6: f5aec5a4...b94, task-7: e91ed094...63d. All prefixed with 'sha256-md:'.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: All convention references in Implementation Notes use the prescribed format. Task 1: 4 conventions with 'Applies: task creates <file> matching the convention's <scope>' format. Task 2: 3 conventions with correct format. Task 3: 1 convention with correct format. Task 4: 2 conventions with correct format. Task 5: 3 conventions with correct format. Task 6: 3 conventions with correct format. No 'Not applicable' annotations found anywhere. Each rationale names a specific file from Files to Modify or Files to Create. Example from task-1: 'Applies: task creates `modules/fundamental/src/remediation/endpoints/summary.rs` matching the convention's Rust module file scope.'

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a priority set (not 'Undefined'), the plan documents that the Feature's priority is inherited by created tasks — evidenced by priority field values in the impact map, task creation log, or task description files. When the Feature's priority is 'Undefined', the plan documents that priority inheritance is skipped (not propagated as null or 'Undefined')
assertion_results.evidence: Feature TC-9006 has priority 'Major' (not 'Undefined'). impact-map.md line 105: 'Priority: inherited from Feature TC-9006 (Major). Priority is not "Undefined", so it is propagated.' Task additional_fields (line 99): '"priority": {"name": "Major"}'. Epic additional_fields also include '"priority": {"name": "Major"}'. summary-comment.md: 'Priority: Major (inherited from Feature TC-9006, propagated to all Epics and tasks)'.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a non-empty fixVersions array and the fixVersion scope config (from ### Jira Field Defaults in CLAUDE.md) is 'task' or 'both' (or absent, defaulting to 'both'), the plan documents that fixVersions are inherited by created tasks — evidenced by fixVersion values in the impact map, task creation log, or task description files. When fixVersion scope is 'feature' or the Feature has no fixVersions, the plan documents that fixVersion inheritance is skipped
assertion_results.evidence: Feature TC-9006 has fixVersions 'RHTPA 1.5.0' (non-empty). CLAUDE.md has no '### Jira Field Defaults' section, so fixVersion scope defaults to 'both'. impact-map.md line 106: 'fixVersions: inherited from Feature TC-9006 (RHTPA 1.5.0). Feature has non-empty fixVersions, and no fixVersion scope setting exists in Jira Field Defaults, so the default "both" applies -- fixVersions are propagated to tasks.' Task additional_fields: '"fixVersions": [{"name": "RHTPA 1.5.0"}]'. summary-comment.md: 'fixVersions: RHTPA 1.5.0 (inherited from Feature TC-9006, propagated to all Epics and tasks; fixVersion scope not configured in Jira Field Defaults, defaulting to "both")'.

assertion_results.text: A summary of inherited field propagation is produced — evidenced by a summary-comment.md file in the outputs directory, or a summary section in the impact map that includes the inherited priority and fixVersion values propagated to tasks, or states they were omitted and why
assertion_results.evidence: summary-comment.md exists in the outputs directory and contains an '## Inherited Field Values' section (lines 33-36) documenting: 'Priority: Major (inherited from Feature TC-9006, propagated to all Epics and tasks)' and 'fixVersions: RHTPA 1.5.0 (inherited from Feature TC-9006, propagated to all Epics and tasks; fixVersion scope not configured in Jira Field Defaults, defaulting to "both")'. The impact-map.md also contains this information in the 'Task additional_fields' section.
